free_text: Polarizations and differential cross sections for the
elastic scattering of 40-MeV protons from C-12, Ca-40,
Ni-58, Zr-90 and Pb-208
A polarized proton beam was produced in either of two
ways: first by utilizing the recoil protons from
alpha-p scattering, and second by the elastic
scattering of protons from Ca.
32 detectors were used simultaneously.
The detectors are 1-in.-diam, 0.29-in.-thick NaI(Tl)
crystal mounted on selected RCA 6199 photomultipliers.
Two scattering chambers were employed in this
experiment. The first was fixed for an angle of
25.5 degr. and served simply as a vacuum container for
the hydrogen or calcium targets used to produced the
polarized beam. The second chamber was the scattering
chamber proper, in which the polarization and
differential-cross section measurements were carried
out. The chamber consists essentially of a 17-in.
diameter vacuum envelope with a circumferential Mylar
window.
The polarized
proton facility described.
Polarization and differential elastic cross-sections
of 59Co; 68Zn; 120Sn.
Inelastic scattering of 40-MeV polarized proton from
Optical model.
Uncertainty in the target thickness.
corrections according to MEMO CP-D/288.
last checking has been done.
Date is corrected
SD:Updated to new date formats, lower case.
Entry was re-compiled.
Carbon of natural isotopic abundance.
The self-supporting metal targets were rolled to about
150 keV thickness for 40-MeV protons, the carbon target
was a 50 mg/cm2 thick machined piece of graphite.
All targets were about 3/4 in. wide and 2 in. tall.
Relative error include all sources of error
except uncertainty in the target thickness.
Tbl.I from Phys.Rev.,147(1966)812
SD: SF9=EXP deleted from REACTION code.
BIB updated. DATA-ERR -> ERR-2. Part of data moved to
Subent 010.
Carbon and calcium were used in their natural isotopic
abundance, Ni-58, Zr-90 and Pb-208 were enriched to
99.95%, 97.8% and 97.98%, respectively, in the desired
isotope. The self-supporting metal targets were rolled
to about 150 keV thickness for 40-MeV protons, the
carbon target was a 50 mg/cm**2 thick machined piece of
graphite. All targets were about 3/4 in. wide and 2 in.
tall.
Statistical errors and the error due to
uncertainties in background subtraction.
Tbl.II from Phys.Rev.,147(1966)812
SD: SF8=ANA added; SF9=EXP deleted from
